articles.title: OF REAL-TIME INSIGHTS
articles.summary: 67% of professionals believe social intelligence will be more influential than traditional market research by 2029. Yet only 10% are equipped to act on social insights in hours.

articles.title: ENVY AND THE FUTURE OF WORK
articles.summary: AI will commoditize production and lower the cost of meeting basic needs. As that happens, value shifts toward what stays scarce: human-driven services, status, and experiences. Gaming shows this clearly, with billions of players spending on cosmetics, communities, and identity rather than access alone. Envy and mimetic desire reflect this shift, since people want what others visibly have. Demand concentrates in high-income elastic categories like luxury and wellness. These areas grow as consumers compare themselves to others and compete for status and exclusivity.

articles.title: THE RE-EMERGENCE OF PRODUCT LINE MARKETING
articles.summary: As more companies break marketing into separate functions, a critical gap is emerging in multi-product organizations. No one ends up owning the integrated story of why a company's products belong together and where the portfolio is heading. Product line marketing fills that gap by sitting between brand and individual product marketing, owning the standard sales deck and social media presence. The role isn't new, but the accelerating trend toward decomposed marketing orgs is making it essential again.

articles.title: COMPETITIVE PRICING STARTS WITH DATA
articles.summary: Competitive pricing depends on continuous market data, not one-time checks. Rising price sensitivity and instant comparison make monitoring essential. Focus on key products like high-volume SKUs and branded items to understand market position. Track price trends, availability, shipping costs, and seller context to get a full picture. Use this data to guide automated repricing and plan around patterns, without reacting to every competitor move.

articles.title: 17 CONTENT TYPES TO SURVIVE GOOGLE'S ZERO-CLICK FUTURE
articles.summary: Google's zero-click era is reducing the value of generic SEO content. With nearly 60% of searches ending without a click, proprietary content, owned audiences, transaction pages, and experience-based formats matter more than traditional blog posts. Focus on original research, creator-led media, community-driven UGC, and tools that help users complete tasks. Trust-building content like brand pages, case studies, documentation, and expert perspectives also plays a larger role.

articles.title: WHY GTM ORCHESTRATION BREAKS WITHOUT CONTACT-LEVEL EXECUTION (WEBINAR)
articles.summary: Speakers from LiveRamp, Reeder, and Influ2 will cover the common ABM orchestration failure points that erode buyer momentum, as well as how contact-level orchestration overcomes them. The webinar will take place on May 6 at 9 AM PT.

articles.title: ASKELEPHANT
articles.summary: AskElephant automates workflows across sales and customer success. It listens to calls, meetings, emails, and CRM activity. It turns that context into actions like CRM updates, follow-ups, tasks, and handoff notes. It also generates alerts for churn and deal risk. It connects with Salesforce, HubSpot, Slack, and Notion.

articles.title: BRAND CONFESSIONAL: RACHEL HIRSCH, WELLNESS GROWTH VENTURES
articles.summary: Strong brands change consumer behavior, not just perception. The focus should shift from aesthetics to repeat actions like weekly purchases, referrals, and organic sharing. Community stands out as the most defensible growth channel since recurring gatherings and active group chats build loyalty that outlasts the product. There is also a clear wellness opportunity in turning intent into default behavior rather than adding more data or dashboards. Early attention should not be mistaken for durable demand.

articles.title: SWIPE OR TAP? HOW AGE SHAPES THE ADOPTION OF NEW TECHNOLOGIES
articles.summary: Age explains nearly 40% of the variation in mobile payment adoption, far exceeding wealth at 7% and occupation at 5%. Usage declines steadily with each decade of age, not just between the youngest and oldest groups. Businesses reflect this pattern, with merchants in younger areas adopting mobile payment terminals about 20% more often than those in older areas. As the global population over 60 is projected to reach 22% by 2050, aging societies may struggle to achieve the network scale needed for digital technologies to deliver full value.

articles.title: VENMO MARKETING STRATEGY: WINNING THE SOCIAL LAYER NO ONE THOUGHT TO BUILD
articles.summary: Peer-to-peer payment apps existed before Venmo, but none added a public social layer. Its feed turned transactions into expressive gestures with emojis and inside jokes, making the app habit-forming. While Cash App leans on celebrity and Zelle stays invisible, Venmo built cultural relevance into the product. Recent campaigns with creators like Alix Earle and White Lotus cast members follow the same playbook, embedding the brand into existing conversations.

articles.title: TOP 9 A/B TESTING PITFALLS
articles.summary: Pitfalls include peeking too early, bad ramp-up analysis, and uncontrolled variants.

articles.title: FAREWELL, JEEVES: ASK.COM SHUTS DOWN
articles.summary: After 25 years of answering the world's questions, Ask.com officially closed on May 1.

articles.title: CHECKOUT PAGE DESIGN: 11 WAYS TO REDUCE CART ABANDONMENT RATES
articles.summary: Tips include displaying the full cost and delivery date on the product page, cutting checkout forms to 6-8 fields, and offering express payment options.
